mechanism_narrative: A rising FEDFUNDS rate transmits tighter discounting and slower risk appetite, so higher policy rates predict negative S&P 500 returns over the next month. The signal is strongest when inflation is already elevated, because real-rate pressure is then more restrictive.
failure_condition: Reject if 20-day forward SPX returns are not lower after FEDFUNDS rises over rolling monthly windows, or if the sign of the relationship turns positive in more than 60% of tightening episodes.

mechanism_narrative: When UNRATE rises by a meaningful amount, it signals weakening income and demand, which should depress equity returns over the next month. The effect should be visible after the unemployment rate breaks above its recent trend, not during flat readings.
failure_condition: Reject if triggered instances do not produce below-baseline 20-day SPX returns, or if positive returns occur in at least 60% of unemployment-shock observations.

mechanism_narrative: A joint slowdown in RSAFS and HOUST indicates demand softening across consumer spending and housing, which should lead to negative S&P 500 returns over roughly one quarter. The effect depends on both series weakening together; either one alone is insufficient.
failure_condition: Reject if simultaneous declines in RSAFS and HOUST do not precede negative 60-day SPX returns more often than not, or if the joint signal loses predictive power in rolling 3-year tests.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating INDPRO with stable or falling CPIAUCSL implies improving real activity without fresh inflation pressure, which supports higher equity prices over the next month. The signal fails when industrial output improves but inflation re-accelerates at the same time.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL growth stays at or below its 3-month average
failure_condition: Reject if positive INDPRO surprises paired with contained CPIAUCSL do not produce above-baseline 20-day SPX returns, or if the sign turns negative in more than 40% of triggered cases.

mechanism_narrative: A persistent rise in GDPC1 reflects stronger aggregate output and earnings capacity, so it predicts positive S&P 500 returns over the following quarter. The effect is broader than monthly noise and should survive when GDP is above its recent trend.
failure_condition: Reject if quarterly GDP growth above trend fails to precede positive 60-day SPX returns, or if the rolling rank correlation remains below 0.03 over any 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: When FEDFUNDS is above a restrictive threshold, the market should react negatively to additional policy tightening because higher short rates raise the equity discount rate and pressure valuations. This signal is active only in a tightening regime and should fade when rates are already near neutral.
failure_condition: Reject if short-horizon SPX returns are not negative after FEDFUNDS is above 4.5%, or if the conditional hit rate is not materially below 50% in restrictive periods.

mechanism_narrative: A combination of falling PAYEMS and rising UNRATE identifies a genuine labor-market deterioration that should pressure equity returns over the next month. The signal is interaction-driven because payroll softness without rising unemployment is less informative than the pair together.
failure_condition: Reject if joint PAYEMS decline and UNRATE increase do not precede negative 20-day SPX returns more often than isolated labor moves, or if positive returns dominate triggered samples.

mechanism_narrative: A rise in HOUST after a prior contraction signals stabilization in interest-sensitive demand and a future rebound in equity sentiment over the next quarter. The effect should be strongest after housing activity has been depressed, because a turn in starts then marks cyclical recovery rather than overheating.
candidate_trigger: HOUST rises above its 3-month moving average after a prior 3-month decline
failure_condition: Reject if housing upturn triggers do not lead to positive 60-day SPX returns, or if the signal reverses sign in a majority of post-decline housing recoveries.
